title: calorophus fasciculatus
intro: Calorophus fasciculatus, also known as the Mexican gold poppy, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and improving sleep quality, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Calorophus fasciculatus are typically made from the plant's roots, leaves, and flowers, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
